key_insights.insight: Couples need "otherness" to maintain desire, which involves having separate identities and experiencing each other outside the home environment.
key_insights.quote: The otherness thing is all about the close couples kind of become like Tweedledum and Tweedledee. They don't do anything separately, but you need to have separateness from your partner.

key_insights.insight: Women tend to experience desire responsively, meaning they need stimulation to become aroused, while men often have spontaneous desire.
key_insights.quote: Responsive desire means that you have no desire for sex or very little desire for sex until somebody is actually doing something to you sexually.

key_insights.insight: Many women don't orgasm from penetrative sex, so focusing solely on intercourse can lead to boredom and decreased desire for women.
key_insights.quote: 20% of women can climax through penetrative sex. That means 80% of women are not having their orgasms through intercourse.

key_insights.insight: Talking openly and specifically about sexual desires and needs is crucial for resolving issues and improving sexual satisfaction in relationships.
key_insights.quote: Every sex problem can be solved if you talk about it. If you don't talk about sex, the tiniest sex problem can ruin your whole sex life.

key_insights.insight: Maintaining one's physical and intellectual attractiveness is part of the obligation within monogamy to sexually satisfy one's partner.
key_insights.quote: If I pledge monogamy, I pledge that I'm going to sexually satisfy my partner. I think you have an obligation to do that. And I'm going to keep myself looking good because love is, you know, kind, but it's not blind.

key_insights.insight: The decline of sex in society is linked to the overabundance of entertainment options and less face-to-face communication, which makes intimacy scary.
key_insights.quote: There wasn't a sex recession before social media, streaming, phones. It's all to do with that. We have too much to do. We basically just go off sex because we have other things to entertain us.

key_insights.insight: Body image significantly impacts sexual desire, enjoyment, and orgasm, but having more sex can improve body image by reinforcing subconscious positive feedback.
key_insights.quote: Body image is terrible...it impacts every single area of sex, regularity of sex, enjoyment of sex, arousal, desire, orgasm.

key_insights.insight: In long-term relationships, it's vital to stage a crisis and openly discuss sexual dissatisfaction before it leads to cheating or separation.
key_insights.quote: You need to say, stop. Like this relationship has to stop. We have to have a conversation now because I'm at a point now where I'm either going to leave this relationship or I'm going to end up cheating or something. So we need to fix this together. And it needs to feel important.

key_insights.insight: Erectile dysfunction can be a major psychological issue for men, and the use of Viagra by young men is creating unrealistic expectations about erections.
key_insights.quote: Erectile dysfunction for men is women don't i appreciate how having not being able to get an erection or you know is is the biggest psychological catastrophe men experience.

key_insights.insight: If the partner is unwilling to sexually compromise or work toward solutions, the relationship may be unsalvageable.
key_insights.quote: If your partner says, I don't want to have sex with you and I have no interest in having sex,I've got no interest in trying to get back my desire and you're not allowedto have sex either you're not allowed to seek it anywhere else...well what choice have you got.

actionable_advice.advice: Initiate sex more often to feel more powerful and boost sexual self-esteem.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take turns initiating; if your partner initiates more often, ask them to hold off so you can initiate and feel sexier.

actionable_advice.advice: Give your partner very specific instructions during sex.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of saying 'this isn't working,' describe your ideal sex session in detail ('Start with kissing, then kiss my neck...') and explain the areas that feel good.

actionable_advice.advice: If your partner wants to try something new in the bedroom that you don't want to do, offer a compromise.
actionable_advice.how_to: If your partner wants to have sex outside but you don't, say that is not your thing, but you're open to trying X instead.

actionable_advice.advice: When discussing sex with your partner, use the 'compliment sandwich'.
actionable_advice.how_to: Start with a compliment about your sex life, then suggest something you'd like more of, and end with another compliment.

actionable_advice.advice: If sex has stopped in your relationship, have the conversation to start little bite-sized pieces of sex.
actionable_advice.how_to: Have a big snogging session, have something where he gives you oral, you don't do anything back, or you give him oral, do something sensual together, have a bath together (that counts as sex). Don't have intercourse, which is the least favorite bit for women, take intercourse out.

memorable_stories: The host shares a story about his partner saying she didn't like having sex which caused them to break up. But a year later she understood what was really going on, which allowed them to be patient enough to rebuild and experiment and their sex life is now the best it has ever been.

The guest shares an anecdote about being on holiday and a woman telling her that she hadn't had good sex in a long time, but after reading the book 'Fifty Shades of Grey', she was excited to have erotic sex with her partner again.
The guest shares an anecdote about how she hit menopause and she no longer felt the desire to have sex, which encouraged her to write her book 'Great Sex Starts at 50'.
contrarian_claims: Monogamy, for the purpose of sexual satisfaction, is not natural.
Love does not blind, and it is rude to think that your partner will never cheat on you no matter how you treat them.
Romance is not what women want, erotic wild sex is.
High achieving women are single because they're too intelligent.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The No.1 Sex Expert: How To Have Great Sex EVERY Time! (And Fix Bad Sex) - Tracey Cox
